items.title: 10-year Treasury yield jumps to 4.57% as jumping oil prices reignite inflation fears
items.summary: Treasury yields surged after President Donald Trump told the NATO summit that the ceasefire with Iran is over.

items.title: Fed meeting minutes to show 'family fight' over rates. The squabble could drag on for a while
items.summary: There have been few instances over the past 35 years or so when the Fed has only made one rate move, be it up or down.

items.title: Bezos' Blue Origin valued at $130 billion in first outside fundraising round
items.summary: Bezos' Blue Origin is raising outside capital in a round valuing the rocket company at $130 billion, sources told CNBC's Andrew Ross Sorkin.

items.title: Rupee sees worst single-day fall in a month
items.summary: G-Sec yields spike as crude oil prices harden

items.title: Government starts industry consultations on next phase of food processing PLI
items.summary: The government is planning an expanded Production-Linked Incentive Scheme for the food processing sector. Consultations were held with industry stakeholders to design the next phase of fiscal support. The upcoming policy aims to boost domestic manufacturing and global competitiveness. It will focus on innovation, value addition, and farmer benefits. Working groups will address perceptions and promote the sector's growth.

items.title: Wall Street slumps after Trump says Iran deal is ‘over’
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.31%, the S&P 500 fell 0.36%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.44%

items.title: Nalco, NLCIL sign JV pact to set up 1,080 MW captive power plant in Odisha
items.summary: National Aluminium Company and NLC India have signed a joint venture agreement. They will jointly establish a 1,080 MW thermal captive power plant in Odisha. This new plant will supply essential power to Nalco's aluminium smelter expansion project. The collaboration also aims to explore renewable energy and secure coal supply. This strategic partnership will enhance Nalco's power generation capabilities.

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'over,' U.S. will 'hit them hard tonight'
items.summary: The president was asked about the ceasefire after an escalation of fighting in the Middle East.

items.title: Broadcom shares jump over 2% after Apple announces $30 billion chip deal
items.summary: Broadcom shares gained after Apple announced a multi-year chip supply agreement exceeding $30 billion. The deal expands US manufacturing, strengthens Broadcom’s custom silicon business through 2031 and supports Apple’s strategy to build a domestic semiconductor supply chain.

items.title: Markets tumble after Donald Trump ends ceasefire
items.summary: The Nifty 50 fell 2.1% to 23,882, while the Sensex fell 2.1% to 76,503—their lowest levels since 30 March.

items.title: IMF lowers India’s FY27 growth outlook, retains status as fastest-growing economy
items.summary: The IMF projects India's FY27 growth at 6.4 percent, remaining a leading global economy. Strong private consumption and services activity support this projected economic expansion. Global growth forecasts for 2026 and 2027 have seen adjustments due to various factors. The United States economy is expected to grow steadily, supported by fiscal policy. China's growth forecast is upgraded, though challenges remain for its economy.

items.title: Maruti Suzuki installs 1 MWh battery storage system at Kharkhoda facility
items.summary: Maruti Suzuki commissioned a one MWh Battery Energy Storage System at its Kharkhoda facility. This system stores surplus solar power generated during low-demand periods. The new BESS will help stabilize the local electricity grid effectively. This initiative aligns with India's focus on building self-reliant energy ecosystems. The company remains committed to lowering emissions despite increasing production volumes.

items.title: Bond yields jump, rupee weakens as oil surges after Trump says Iran deal is over
items.summary: The rise in crude oil prices renewed concerns over inflation and India’s external balances.

items.title: Multibagger small-cap Cupid logs biggest one-day fall in 6 months; ends 3-day rally
items.summary: Cupid shares fell 11% on July 8, reversing a strong 14-session winning streak. After hitting a record high of ₹226, the stock plunged to ₹197, marking its biggest single-day decline since January. Retail investors benefited significantly from its 1,416% rally over the past years.

items.title: Apple loses spat with EU over App Store and iPhone rules
items.summary: Judges at the EU’s General Court in Luxembourg disagreed with Apple’s challenge to the services being targeted under the EU’s Digital Markets Act, saying regulators were right to draw the two services under the scope of the rules

items.title: India's Iran oil plans in a quandary after Trump declares US-Iran ceasefire over
items.summary: US President Donald Trump’s declaration on Wednesday that the memorandum of understanding with Tehran is effectively over—coupled with Washington’s decision to end a sanctions waiver on Iranian energy supplies—has clouded the prospects of oil imports

items.title: HFCL to invest Rs 950 crore in optical fibre cable production in next 2 years: MD
items.summary: While announcing the launch of the HFCL's data centre solution portfolio, OptiQ AI, its managing director Mahendra Nahata said that there is a huge demand for optical fibre cables in the data centre segment, especially in the US, which is driving growth for the industry.

items.title: US stocks today: Dow Jones crashes 500 points at open as Trump stops Iran peace deal
items.summary: Wall Street opened lower after Trump said the Iran deal was over, triggering a risk-off sentiment globally. Rising oil prices added pressure on equities, with Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipping at the open as investors reacted to geopolitical uncertainty and potential inflation risks.

items.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

items.title: US and Iran trade strikes after attacks on tankers
items.summary: Donald Trump says he thinks ceasefire deal with Tehran is ‘over’ following round of tit-for-tat bombing

items.title: Rising battery costs seen pushing up India storage tariffs as developers warn on project viability
items.summary: Battery storage tariffs in India are anticipated to increase significantly. Rising input costs and reduced Chinese incentives are pushing up battery prices. This situation makes previously low-priced projects difficult to sustain. Some funded projects have stalled as battery prices increased unexpectedly. Realistic tariffs are needed to ensure project viability and quality standards.

items.title: Severn Trent avoids fine for wastewater failures
items.summary: The water firm took "genuine accountability" after breaching wastewater obligations, Ofwat says.

items.title: Torrent Pharma, JB Chemicals hit 52-week highs after NCLT approves merger
items.summary: The companies said the amalgamation under Sections 230 to 232 of the Companies Act will become effective after filing the certified NCLT order with the Registrar of Companies.

items.title: IMF sees India among fastest-growing major economies despite trimming FY27 GDP growth projection to 6.4%
items.summary: India's economy is anticipated to expand by 6.4 percent in fiscal year 2026-27, bolstered by strong private consumption and active service sectors. Although global growth is predicted to ease to 3.0 percent in 2026, domestic demand is set to drive India's economic growth amidst international uncertainties. The Reserve Bank of India also predicts a vibrant economic landscape for the country.

items.title: IMF lowers 2026 global growth outlook to 3%, projects 3.4% growth in 2027
items.summary: The International Monetary Fund has adjusted its global growth prediction for 2026, reflecting the adverse effects of warfare and rapid technological changes. Inflation is anticipated to rise before stabilizing later in the year. Furthermore, trade volumes are forecasted to experience a notable decline in growth, with a rebound expected afterward. Overall, the risks associated with the economic outlook remain skewed toward negative developments.

items.title: Europe must have the financial power to match its economic heft
items.summary: The continent’s savings should be used to invest in its own future, not someone else’s

items.title: Jute stock curbs withdrawn as record acreage signals end of shortage
items.summary: The Jute Commissioner's office has withdrawn raw jute stock control orders immediately. This action removes holding limits for traders and mills as the new jute season begins. Expectations point towards a significantly larger jute crop this year, easing previous scarcity concerns. Forward prices have already decreased substantially, reflecting improved market sentiment and availability. Mills can now carry necessary working stock while maintaining real-time reporting on a new portal.

items.title: Jackdaw owner says gas field will 'not materially influence' climate change
items.summary: The new assessment was required by the industry regulator, after it found several areas had not been adequately addressed in a previous submission.

items.title: Apple commits over $30 billion to Broadcom to produce billions more US chips
items.summary: Apple has announced an expansion of its partnership with Broadcom, involving over $30 billion in investments. This agreement will facilitate the production of 15 billion US-made chips. Details here.

items.title: Kusumgar IPO: Issue received a solid response on Day 01, booked 3.7 times; GMP signals bumper listing
items.summary: Kusumgar Limited's IPO saw robust interest on its first day, subscribing 3.67 times with 3.99 crore shares bid against 1.08 lakh available. Non-institutional investors led the demand. The issue closes July 10, with a price band set between ₹398 and ₹419.

items.title: UniCredit secures 48% stake in Commerzbank
items.summary: Italian bank closes in on majority control of German lender after end of tender period

items.title: Tata Group targets doubling automotive revenue to $100 billion in five years
items.summary: Tata Motors targets doubling automotive revenue to $100 billion within five years. Jaguar Land Rover will contribute significantly to this ambitious revenue goal. The commercial vehicle segment is also projected for substantial growth and increased revenue. Tata Motors plans significant investments and new models for its domestic operations. The company aims for increased market share and profitability across its automotive businesses.

items.title: HSBC drops 'overweight' call on EM equities on AI spending fears
items.summary: HSBC has reassessed its position on emerging markets equities, shifting to an 'overweight' stance due to rising volatility in Asian markets and ongoing concerns about AI-related expenditures. South Korean stocks have notably suffered, dipping into bear market conditions. Investors are growing wary of the sustainability of AI investments backed by debt. Meanwhile, HSBC has raised the rating on eurozone equities to 'overweight,' highlighting favorable market factors.

items.title: Amit Shah directs immigration checkpoints at Jewar, Agartala airports to open soon
items.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah directed operationalization of immigration checkpoints at Agartala and Jewar airports. He also prioritized development of sixty-two airports within two years for passenger convenience. Foreigners Regional Registration Offices will become operational at all state headquarters by 2027. A mechanism will be established to promote the Fast Track Immigration Programme with airlines. Automated X-ray Tray Return Systems will be installed nationwide to reduce human resource costs.

items.title: Ladakh tourism surges as June arrivals jump 43% and foreign visitors double from 2025
items.summary: Ladakh experiences a significant tourism surge, with visitor numbers climbing substantially. Foreign tourist arrivals have nearly doubled, indicating growing international interest in the region. This growth fuels economic confidence for local businesses and residents. Policy changes and expanded amenities have facilitated this impressive tourism expansion. Niche and climate-resilient tourism offerings are now being promoted throughout the year.

items.title: RBI reiterates push for crypto 'prohibition', cites financial risks and tax evasion
items.summary: Several key government agencies continue to favour stricter controls on virtual digital assets, even as India has not yet finalised a clear regulatory framework for cryptocurrencies.

items.title: Quote of the day by Henry Singleton: "Our accounting is set to maximize cash flow, not reported earnings. Smoothing reported earnings just has to take a backseat"
items.summary: Henry Singleton emphasized cash flow over reported earnings for business success. Sustainable businesses generate strong cash, not just accounting profits. Management should focus on economic value, not quarterly earnings targets. Investors gain deeper insights by examining operating and free cash flow. Cash generation remains the essential lifeblood of any business.

items.title: Alliance Insurance Brokers helps settle Rs 1,100-crore health insurance claims in FY26
items.summary: Alliance Insurance Brokers settled over 1.5 lakh group health claims in FY26. The company disbursed a total of Rs 1,100 crore for these claims. Ninety-six percent of claims were settled within ten working days. Their employee benefits products now cover 5.8 million lives. Managed premiums reached Rs 1,600 crore during the year.

items.title: Outcry as Meta lets users make AI images from public Instagram profile pics
items.summary: The tech giant said people can opt out - but privacy campaigners called it a "recipe for disaster".

items.title: Power transmission projects to face execution risks on land acquisition, regulatory approval challenges: Icra
items.summary: Power transmission projects face significant execution risks and delays. Land acquisition and regulatory approvals hinder timely project completion. These delays impact renewable energy developers and their financial returns. The sector expects substantial capacity additions by 2032. However, execution challenges persist for future projects.

items.title: Maize prices likely to rule at ₹2,400 a quintal, says Tamil Nadu Agricultural University
items.summary: The varsity’s Centre for Agricultural and Rural Development Studies has advised farmers in TNmil Nadu to make informed sowing decision

items.title: Heritage Foods says 'Fresh Paneer' label is descriptive, responds to FSSAI notice
items.summary: Heritage Foods responded to FSSAI's show-cause notice concerning its 'Fresh Paneer' product label. The company stated the term accurately describes the dairy product's inherent nature. It clarified that 'Fresh Paneer' refers to refrigerated consumption with a limited shelf life. Heritage Foods also addressed the 'Health and Happiness' slogan as a corporate philosophy. The dairy firm reaffirmed its commitment to food safety and regulatory compliance.

items.title: Thali costs rose by 5% in June on higher tomato, onion, LPG prices
items.summary: Home-cooked thali costs increased in June due to higher vegetable and fuel prices. Tomato and onion prices saw significant year-on-year increases, impacting meal expenses. Vegetable oil and LPG costs also rose, contributing to the overall price hike. Potato prices declined, partially offsetting the rising costs of other ingredients.

items.title: Nuvama, Cushman & Wakefield-backed realty fund raises Rs 4,000 cr
items.summary: The NCW real estate fund has triumphant news as it successfully closes its PRIME Offices Fund at a hefty Rs 4,000 crore. This collaboration between Nuvama and Cushman & Wakefield has not only met but exceeded its initial fundraising goal. With 45% of its capital already allocated to office properties, the fund will enhance access for Indian investors to premium commercial office spaces in key metropolitan areas.

items.title: US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures drop up to 1% as oil rally and Middle East risks hit sentiment
items.summary: US stock futures indicated a weak start on 8 July amid rising crude oil prices and tensions in the Middle East. S&P 500 futures fell 0.8%, while Dow Jones and Nasdaq futures declined 1%. Investor sentiment weakened following US strikes on Iran amid escalating conflict.

items.title: Dow Jones| Nasdaq | US Stock Market Today | Live: Dow Jones crashes 500 points at open as Trump stops Iran peace deal
India's 10-year bond logs worst day in 3 months on faltering Iran deal
items.summary: India's benchmark bond faced a dramatic decline, its most significant drop in over three months, primarily driven by a surge in oil prices linked to geopolitical tensions. This alarming rise in crude prices threatens India's economic growth and inflation forecast, leading to a substantial uptick in bond yields and swap rates. Despite these challenges, foreign investors remain consistently interested in India's debt markets.

items.title: India–UK FTA to boost gem and jewellery exports as it comes into force on July 15
items.summary: India's gem and jewellery exports to the UK are expected to triple after the FTA begins. The agreement grants duty-free access for nearly ninety-nine percent of Indian exports. This will enhance the price competitiveness of Indian jewellery in the British market. The Gem and Jewellery Export Promotion Council anticipates exports reaching two point five billion dollars. This trade pact aims to expand overall bilateral trade between the two nations.

items.title: Geopolitical tensions sink markets; Nifty posts worst fall in over three months
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices logged their worst session in over three months after Donald Trump declared the Iran ceasefire over, sending crude oil prices soaring, the rupee lower and the India VIX sharply higher amid broad-based selling across sectors

items.title: Government notifies Navi Mumbai Airport as a Port for Import of drugs under Drug Rules
items.summary: The Ministry of Health has enabled drug imports through Navi Mumbai Airport. This amendment strengthens the pharmaceutical supply chain and trade facilitation. Navi Mumbai Airport now joins other designated ports for drug importation. The total number of entry points for drugs has increased to forty-two. This initiative promotes ease of doing business and regulatory oversight.

items.title: Godrej Agrovet to set up Rs 300-cr oil palm complex in Telangana
items.summary: Godrej Agrovet is establishing an integrated oil palm complex in Telangana. This significant investment of Rs 300 crore will create over 700 new livelihood opportunities. The state government has allocated 113.5 acres of land for this major project. This land will house a processing unit, nursery, R&D centre, and seed garden. The Chief Minister has directed officials to facilitate necessary road expansion for the mill.

items.title: Gillco Group to invest Rs 800 crore to develop a premium residential project in Mohali
items.summary: Gillco Group's development plan will feature 444 residences across six towers on 12 acres. Construction has commenced, with completion expected within four years under RERA timelines. Homes are priced from approximately Rs 11,500 per square foot.

items.title: Punjab & Sind Bank gets approval to set up IFSC banking unit at GIFT City
items.summary: This marks a step in the bank's journey towards expanding its international banking presence and offering world-class offshore banking services, Punjab & Sind Bank said in a statement.

items.title: What are the risks of an LNG tanker explosion?
items.summary: A Qatari LNG tanker sustained damage in an attack near the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel, Al Rekayyat, is reportedly at risk of exploding following the incident. This marks the second war-related attack on an LNG carrier this year. LNG is natural gas cooled to liquid form for easier transport. Modern LNG carriers have multiple safety features to prevent explosions.

items.title: Backlash on ethanol-blend fuel intensifies in India, puts carmakers in the dock
items.summary: India's mandatory E20 fuel policy faces public backlash and political opposition. Car owners report reduced mileage and potential vehicle damage from the new fuel. The government asserts E20 reduces crude oil imports and aids farmers. Opposition leaders demand clarity from automakers regarding vehicle compatibility with E20. This policy, implemented nationwide, has reignited concerns about its rapid rollout.

items.title: Oil jumps over 6% as Trump declares Iran ceasefire, MoU effectively over
items.summary: The US has reinstated sanctions on Iranian oil and launches fresh strikes, reviving fears of disruption in the Strait of Hormuz and raising fresh concerns for India's energy security and inflation outlook.

items.title: Indian-flagged crude tanker turns back from Strait of Hormuz after vessel attacks: Report
items.summary: Several oil and gas tankers have turned back from the Strait of Hormuz. This action follows renewed attacks on vessels in the critical waterway. A Qatari LNG tanker and a Saudi crude oil tanker were damaged near the strait. Maritime authorities have raised the threat risk for transiting vessels to severe. Indian refiner Mangalore Refinery cancelled a crude oil charter from Iraq.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Minda Corp (Buy)
items.summary: Monarch Networth Capital

items.title: India seeks safe Hormuz passage for nine ships as truce falters
items.summary: In a strategic move, India is engaging Iran in talks to guarantee the safety of nine tankers loaded with crude oil and liquefied petroleum gas. With vigilant oversight from the government on these vessels, officials will confer with Iranian counterparts to discuss the precarious state of maritime security due to regional tensions that threaten both shipments and a delicate ceasefire.

items.title: Sebi amends rule for foreign investors, directs registration fee payment in Indian rupee
items.summary: Sebi has announced a modification to its foreign investor registration fees, shifting to a rupee-based model effective in six months. This adjustment stipulates that Category-I FPIs and FVCIs will be required to pay Rs 2.3 lakh. Additionally, the common application will now demand the date of birth from applicants, and depository participants must ensure that all fees are forwarded to Sebi within a five-day period.

items.title: GJEPC prepares ground for FTA with UK from July 15
items.summary: GJEPC estimates that India’s gem and jewellery exports to the UK can more than triple from $2.5 billion to $7 billion over the coming years on the back of bilateral trade

items.title: Amazon Seller Services leases over 4.17 lakh sq ft warehouse in Bhiwandi near Mumbai
items.summary: Amazon India has taken a warehousing facility in Bhiwandi through a long-term lease, set for nine years beginning June 2026. The financial commitment involves a monthly rent exceeding Rs 1.27 crore. This strategic move highlights Amazon's commitment to enhancing its logistics infrastructure and fortifying its supply chain.

items.title: Biometric UPI transactions top 600 million in June, says NPCI
items.summary: Biometric UPI transactions surpassed 600 million in June, indicating strong user preference. This secure authentication method reduces reliance on UPI PINs for faster payments. The feature supports various transactions, including RuPay Credit Card on UPI payments. Banks and UPI apps are increasingly adopting this convenient and efficient payment solution. Wider adoption is expected to further solidify UPI's position as India's preferred digital platform.

items.title: Virgin Media fined after hanging up on customers trying to cancel contracts
items.summary: Millions of phone calls from customers were "likely mishandled" over nearly a three-year period, the regulator says.

items.title: EV push threatens CNG and PNG market as city gas firms brace for volume and price pressure: Report
items.summary: Electric vehicles are gaining ground fast in key city gas areas and the surge is starting to eat into CNG market share and could weigh on long-term PNG demand too, according to a report by brokerage firm Dolat Capital. This comes in contrast to City Gas Distributors cutting prices to defend market share against propane.

items.title: Why AI literacy is the most important skill for students entering the job market
items.summary: Employers now expect AI-ready graduates. This article explores why AI literacy is essential for students and how ET Masterclass's new program bridges the classroom-to-career skills gap.

items.title: Crude oil price surges more than 6% after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'over'
items.summary: Brent crude price jumps 6.3% to $78.80 a barrel

items.title: Microsoft layoff: Employees offered up to 39 weeks' pay, stock vesting; here's who gets what
items.summary: Microsoft announced layoffs affecting 4,800 employees, offering severance packages up to 39 weeks' pay. Compensation varies by employee level, with options for continued stock vesting and health insurance. The cuts aim to streamline costs amid significant AI investments.

items.title: Inside the world’s biggest battery maker
items.summary: What does CATL’s extraordinary rise mean for the world?

items.title: India silver import curbs create shortages, push premiums to six-month high
items.summary: India's silver import restrictions have caused significant shortages in the domestic market. Premiums have surged to their highest levels in six months due to these curbs. May imports saw a drastic fall from the previous year's figures. These measures aim to ease pressure on foreign exchange reserves and the rupee. Domestic supplies are now largely dependent on local production.

items.title: DPR underway for Ghaziabad-Jewar RRTS corridor to cut travel time to Noida airport
items.summary: The Ghaziabad-Jewar Namo Bharat corridor has advanced to the Detailed Project Report stage. This new rapid transit line will connect Ghaziabad to Noida International Airport. The corridor is planned to feature twelve stations and two depots along its route. It aims to significantly reduce travel times between key NCR cities and the airport.

items.title: Trump pours cold water on NATO allies' united front
items.summary: Trump criticized NATO allies over defense spending, attacked Spain and renewed his Greenland push during the alliance’s summit in Turkey.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on 8 July: Aegis Logistics, HPCL, Bank of India, IndiGo, Coforge, MRF among top losers
items.summary: The Indian stock market faced heavy selling on Wednesday, with benchmark indices falling over 2% amid renewed U.S.-Iran tensions. items.title: Sugar prices up 6-7% in June amid below-normal rainfall
items.summary: Sugar prices have increased six to seven percent over June. This rise occurred as below-normal rainfall raised concerns about crop output. Demand also climbed with the end of the Hindu month of Adhik Maas. Ex-mill sugar prices in Maharashtra have increased to about forty-one point five rupees per kilogram. Recent rainfall is expected to provide relief to the standing sugarcane crop.

items.title: Kharif sowing slow, 15 more districts face rain deficiency
items.summary: The government is monitoring 262 districts for rainfall deficiency and El Nino impacts. Fifteen additional districts have been identified as facing rainfall deficits. Sowing remains slower than usual due to uneven rainfall across the country. However, rains are forecast for Gujarat, Haryana, Punjab, and Rajasthan soon. Contingency plans are being implemented to minimize potential impacts on agriculture.

items.title: India asks US to reconsider proposed 12.5% additional tariff
items.summary: India has asked the US to reconsider proposed additional tariffs on its exports. New Delhi argues the proposed tariffs lack legal basis and sufficient evidence. The US Trade Representative is investigating dozens of economies for forced labor import policies. India believes the US has not conducted economy-specific analyses of these practices. Dialogue is preferred to address any specific concerns raised by the US.

items.title: Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister calls for rapid scale-up of grid-scale battery storage
items.summary: India's electricity challenge has shifted from generation to managing availability and flexibility. Rising solar power causes grid stress and price volatility between day and night. The government recognises energy storage as crucial for the power system. Expanding solar capacity needs corresponding investments in storage solutions. This ensures grid stability and meets evening peak demand effectively.

items.title: Information & Computer services to make up 1/5th of India's proposed planned index
